writeup_note_markdown:
# Electroweak and Cabibbo Note

## Objective

This note freezes the current bounded electroweak-scale bridge and Cabibbo
mixing result into one manuscript-safe artifact.

## Locked Classifications

- `electroweak_scale_geometric_bridge_supported`
- `cabibbo_angle_topology_supported`

## Electroweak Scale Bridge

The best bounded polynomial bridge from the microscopic lattice scale to the
electroweak scale is:

- `v = mu_0 * (5*248 + 8)`
- `mu_0 = 197.3269804 MeV`
- predicted `v = 246264.0715 MeV`
- predicted `M_W = 80169.3769 MeV`
- predicted `M_Z = 90721.6199 MeV`

| Observable | Predicted value (MeV) | Target value (MeV) | Relative error |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| `vev` | 246264.0715 | 246220.0000 | 0.0179% |
| `mw` | 80169.3769 | 80379.0000 | 0.2608% |
| `mz` | 90721.6199 | 91187.6000 | 0.5110% |

The manuscript-safe interpretation is:

> The electroweak VEV behaves like a macroscopic scaling of the microscopic
> lattice scale `mu_0` through pure `E8` invariants. In the current bounded
> search, the cleanest bridge is `5*248 + 8`, i.e. five copies of the adjoint
> dimension plus the eight-dimensional Cartan rank.

## Cabibbo Mixing

The supported bounded Cabibbo relation is:

- `tan(theta_C) ~= 3 * ((Delta_2 - Delta_1) / Delta_2) * sqrt(delta_branch)`
- predicted `tan(theta_C) = 0.2307896962`
- target `tan(theta_C) = 0.2309211026`
- relative error = `0.0569%`
- predicted `theta_C = 13.3436 deg`

This is stronger than the best pure branch-only competitor
`sin(theta_C) ~= 7 * delta_branch`, and it means the first-two-generation weak
gap structure is not optional in the current lock.

## Interpretation

The combined manuscript-safe conclusion is:

> The electroweak VEV, the `W/Z` masses, and the Cabibbo mixing angle all admit
> bounded geometric reconstructions from the same `E8` lattice-scale and
> weak-gap framework.

This note promotes:

- the electroweak scale bridge from `mu_0` to `v`
- the bounded `W/Z` mass predictions
- the bounded topological quantization of the Cabibbo angle

This note does **not** promote:

- a full Higgs-sector derivation
- a complete CKM matrix derivation
- a unique electroweak completion beyond the currently tested bounded formulas
